Possible Causes: Rahjamal Traps volcanism, associated with Kerguelen hotspot

Possible Causes: Global climate change, evolution of new life forms, extinction of aquatic invertebrates
Characteristics: Shift from arid to humid climate, first appearance of dinosaurs, lepidosaurs, and calcium-containing microfossils, less carbonate deposition in oceans, chemical changes in strata indicating global warming, possibly linked to flood basalt eruptions

Possible Causes: Large disturbances in carbon, oxygen, and sulfur cycles, positive carbon-isotope event, absence of black shale, 400,000-year interval
Characteristics: Anoxic extinction event in the Cretaceous period, refined boundary at 93.9 Ma, global type section in Colorado, known as the Bonarelli event, presence of radiolarians indicating high productivity

Impacted Organisms: Three-quarters of plant and animal species

Characteristics: Sudden mass extinction, end of Cretaceous period, beginning of Cenozoic Era, K-Pg boundary clay with high iridium levels, simultaneous extinctions indicating asteroid impact

Possible Causes: Climate change, volcanic activity, oceanic anoxia
Characteristics: Decreased species richness, increased extinction rates, impact on marine and terrestrial ecosystems, debate on severity compared to other mass extinctions

Characteristics: Transition to an oxidizing atmosphere, mass extinction of anaerobic species, enabling development of multicellular life-forms

Possible Causes: Human activity, habitat degradation, overhunting, climate change
Characteristics: Ongoing extinction event, high rate of species loss, disappearance of megafauna, correlation with human emergence, potential impact of climate change

Possible Causes: Mechanism originating in deep oceans, impact on shallow shelf seas, extinction lasting 200,000 years, linked to Milankovic cycles, δ13C and δ18O excursions observed

Possible Causes: Changes in sea level, ocean anoxia, global cooling, oceanic volcanism, extraterrestrial impact

Possible Causes: Global low sea level, geochemical isotope excursion, change in depositional regime
Characteristics: Last of three minor Silurian mass extinctions, major effect on conodont fauna, barely impacted graptolites, observed in Gotland, Sweden, local extinctions, peak in δ13C, altered community structures

Possible Causes: Cooling period, growth of global ice sheets, orbitally paced changes in oceanic and atmospheric circulation, drop in atmospheric CO2 levels

Possible Causes: Anoxic event, global drop in sea level, geochemical isotopes excursion, change in depositional environment, sustained drop in CO2 levels
Sudden change in faunas, uncertainty in duration and timing of extinctions, theories of prolonged extinction or multiple pulses
Characteristics: Extinction event in late Cisuralian or early Guadalupian of the Permian period, predated the Permian-Triassic extinction, impact amplified effects of later extinction event, killed off about 80% of species alive at the time

Impacted Organisms: 57% of biological families, 83% of genera, 81% of marine species, 70% of terrestrial vertebrate species
Possible Causes: Elevated temperatures, widespread oceanic anoxia, ocean acidification, emissions from Siberian Traps, thermal decomposition of hydrocarbon deposits, methane emissions
Characteristics: Largest known mass extinction event, extinction of insects, one to three distinct pulses of extinction, disputed speed of recovery, localized quick rebound in some areas, precise dating of extinction event

Possible Causes: Overkill by humans, natural climate change
Characteristics: Collapse in faunal density and diversity, extinction of key ecological strata, ongoing extinctions, debate on separate events or part of the same event

Characteristics: Extinction of conodonts and 23-34% of marine genera, extinction of non-dinosaurian archosauromorphs, dominance of dinosaurs and pterosaurs for the next 135 million years, decrease in diversity caused by speciation decrease, ecological catastrophe indicated by plant spore turnover and coral reef collapse
